summary: Freemium funnel verification — 1920x900 desktop preview URL. PASS: FREEMIUM 1 (signed-out user creates a map via library-new-mindmap → lands on /app, NO RenewWall), FREEMIUM 7 (Pro/?fam67 user shows neither soft nor hard nudge — isPro gate works), LIBRARY HEADER at 1100/1280/1366 (filter buttons all fully on-screen, no overlap with 'Library' title — flex-wrap works), REGRESSION /library?fam67 cold (library-page renders, no AccessGate, no Maintenance splash), REGRESSION /admin/ops cold (gated — shows the Becoming-Marvex maintenance page, admin NOT exposed), REGRESSION /timeline/new?fam67 (wizard opens; 6 designations Student/Professional/Historian/Personal/Project/Custom + 5 palettes Cosmic/Sunrise/Forest/Ocean/Mono visually verified). UNVERIFIED-VIA-SCRIPT: FREEMIUM 2-6 (soft nudge at 24, dismiss, hard nudge at 30, upgrade CTA, later/reload persistence). The Tab-spam approach to add 23+ child nodes was unreliable — Tab focus drifts to the toolbar/sidebar UI when the canvas loses focus, so the script could only push the example-welcome map from 12 to 15 nodes before plateauing. The FreeCapNudge.jsx logic was reviewed statically and is correct (gated on !isPro, nodeCap===FREE_NODE_CAP, dismiss persisted in sessionStorage keyed by mapId). Recommend main-agent verify F2-F6 manually via UI clicks (right-click → 'Add child') or a programmatic test hook that calls addChild N times.

ui_bugs.issue: Console error 'Maximum update depth exceeded' fires intermittently during free-user node-add operations on the welcome example map. Stack points to commitLayoutEffectOnFiber repeatedly traversing layout effects after Tab adds a child. The runtime-error overlay surfaced once (screenshot saved). App recovers, but this indicates a setState-in-useEffect loop somewhere in the mind-map render tree triggered by node mutations on a free-tier session. RCA candidates: (1) FreeCapNudge useEffect that re-reads sessionStorage on dismissKey change — currently safe, deps array OK; (2) parent passing nodeCount={countNodes(map)} which is fine (primitive); (3) more likely: another consumer of nodeCount or license state in the toolbar/properties pane. Worth a closer look in MindMapCanvas around lines 380-790 where countNodes(map) and nodeCap interact.

action_items: Investigate 'Maximum update depth exceeded' triggered after free-user adds a few child nodes on the welcome example map. Likely a setState-in-useEffect feedback loop in a component that watches nodeCount or license tier. Repro: clear marvex.tester.unlocked.v1 (keep mindmapper.unlocked.v1=1) → /library → click MIND-MAP → click root node → press Tab 5-15 times → red overlay appears.

Manually verify FREEMIUM 2-6 (24-node soft nudge, 30-node hard nudge, upgrade CTA, dismiss persistence) by adding children via right-click → 'Add child' on the canvas instead of Tab spam — Tab key navigation from canvas frequently drifts focus to the toolbar/sidebar, making automated Tab-based node creation unreliable.

OPTIONAL: Add a `data-testid='tl-wiz-designation-{id}'` and `data-testid='tl-wiz-palette-{id}'` on the timeline wizard entries so future regression tests can count them programmatically. The wizard renders correctly today (6 designations + 5 palettes verified visually) — this is a testability nit.

OPTIONAL: Library 'MIND-MAP' click lands on /app?example=welcome rather than a fresh empty map for a brand-new free user. Functionally fine (still no RenewWall, F1 passes), but the feature description says 'fresh map'. If the intent is empty-canvas-on-create, that's a small product gap.
critical_code_review_comments: FreeCapNudge.jsx is clean and well-commented. Gating is correct (isPro early-return, nodeCap === FREE_NODE_CAP, threshold = FREE_NODE_CAP - 6, dismissed via sessionStorage keyed by mapId so it re-shows in a new map). No issues found in this file.

MindMapCanvas.jsx now renders <FreeCapNudge nodeCount={countNodes(map)} ... /> on every render. countNodes is a recursive walk; for very large maps this is fine at 30 nodes but worth memoizing with useMemo([map]) once maps grow.

The 'Maximum update depth' overlay observed during testing is the only blocker found. Suggest checking whether any useEffect in MindMapCanvas / Studio.jsx watches nodeCount or computed license state and unconditionally setStates back into a value those effects depend on.
library-new-mindmap on first-time free user lands on /app?example=welcome — confirm that's intentional product behaviour vs. always-empty new map.

success_rate.frontend: FREEMIUM 1 PASS, FREEMIUM 7 PASS, LIBRARY HEADER 1100/1280/1366 PASS, REGRESSION /library cold PASS, REGRESSION /admin/ops gated PASS, REGRESSION /timeline/new wizard renders correctly. FREEMIUM 2-6 NOT script-verified (Tab focus drift + Max-update-depth runtime error blocked the 24/30-node target).

test_credentials: ?fam67 query param (any URL) → marvex.tester.unlocked.v1 + mindmapper.unlocked.v1 set in localStorage. To simulate FREE: visit /?fam67 then page.evaluate localStorage.removeItem('marvex.tester.unlocked.v1') keeping mindmapper.unlocked.v1='1'.
seed_data_creation: None — used in-app demo maps (Welcome to mind-mapper, Pride and Prejudice, The Republic, How to use Marvex Studio, Guide). No persisted writes during this run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: FREEMIUM 1, 7, LIBRARY HEADER (3 viewports), and 3 REGRESSION cases all PASS. The remaining 2-6 cases require reliably driving the canvas to 24/30 nodes — Tab-spam is unreliable because focus drifts. Use right-click context menu → 'Add child' or wire a temporary window.__addChildren(n) hook. Watch for the 'Maximum update depth' runtime error which surfaces after 5-15 Tab cycles on the welcome example map for free users; reproduce, then narrow it to the offending useEffect (suspect: anything in MindMapCanvas or its descendants that watches nodeCount/license). The 'A small, honest ask' analytics banner appears bottom-right on every fresh session — click DECLINE to clear it.
rca of the issue: Reproduction of 'Maximum update depth exceeded': (1) /?fam67 (2) localStorage.removeItem('marvex.tester.unlocked.v1') (3) /library → click [data-testid='library-new-mindmap'] (4) skip welcome tour (5) click [data-testid='mm-node-example-welcome'] (6) press Tab 5-15 times. Result: red runtime-error overlay 'Maximum update depth exceeded. This can happen when a component repeatedly calls setState inside componentWillUpdate or componentDidUpdate.' Stack trace shows commitLayoutEffectOnFiber → recursivelyTraverseLayoutEffects looping. Suggested root-cause hunt: search MindMapCanvas.jsx and FreeCapNudge consumers for useEffect blocks whose dependency includes a freshly-derived value (object/array) that is recreated every render, or a setState that always fires in the effect body without guard.
